UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the HealthLinke application, storing personal information and linking to their health records and activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

primaryAddressId.description: Reference to the user's primary Address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

FamilyMember.title: FamilyMember
FamilyMember.description: Represents a family member associated with a user's profile, allowing a user to manage health records for dependents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FamilyMember entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this family member belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FamilyMember)

name.description: The full name of the family member.

relationship.description: The relationship of the family member to the primary user (e.g., 'Child', 'Spouse', 'Parent').

dateOfBirth.description: The family member's date of birth.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores physical address details, used for user profiles, delivery, or lab locations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (optional).

state.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code or ZIP code of the address.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the user's default address.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a medical professional available for consultation, including their qualifications and consultation details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

name.description: The full name of the doctor.

photoUrl.description: URL to the doctor's profile photo.

degree.description: The highest medical degree obtained by the doctor.

experienceYears.description: Number of years of experience the doctor has.

fees.description: Consultation fees charged by the doctor.

consultationTypeIds.description: References to ConsultationTypes offered by this doctor. (Relationship: Doctor N:N ConsultationType)

verifiedStatus.description: Indicates if the doctor's credentials have been verified.

specializationIds.description: References to Specializations of the doctor. (Relationship: Doctor N:N Specialization)

clinicAddress.description: The physical address of the doctor's clinic.

phone.description: Contact phone number for the doctor.

email.description: Contact email address for the doctor.

Specialization.title: Specialization
Specialization.description: Defines medical specializations, used to categorize doctors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Specialization entity.

description.description: A brief description of the specialization.

ConsultationType.title: ConsultationType
ConsultationType.description: Defines the types of consultations available through the platform (e.g., Chat, Audio Call, Video Call).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConsultationType entity.

name.description: The name of the consultation type (e.g., 'Chat', 'Audio Call', 'Video Call').

description.description: A description of the consultation type.

TimeSlot.title: TimeSlot
TimeSlot.description: Represents an available or booked time slot for a doctor's consultation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimeSlot entity.

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor offering this time slot. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N TimeSlot)

startTime.description: The start date and time of the slot.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the slot.

isBooked.description: Indicates if the time slot is currently booked.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Records a scheduled or completed consultation between a user and a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile making the appointment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Appointment)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor for this appointment. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Appointment)

timeSlotId.description: Reference to the booked TimeSlot for this appointment. (Relationship: TimeSlot 1:N Appointment)

consultationTypeId.description: Reference to the ConsultationType for this appointment. (Relationship: ConsultationType 1:N Appointment)

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

bookingDate.description: The date and time when the appointment was booked.

followUpRequired.description: Indicates if a follow-up consultation is required.

followUpPaid.description: Indicates if the follow-up consultation is paid or free.

diagnosis.description: The diagnosis provided by the doctor during the consultation.

MedicineCategory.title: MedicineCategory
MedicineCategory.description: Groups medicines by their type (e.g., Tablets, Syrup, Ayurvedic, Baby Care).

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicineCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the medicine category.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Medicine.title: Medicine
Medicine.description: Details about a specific medicine available for purchase in the online store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Medicine entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the medicine, its uses, and side effects.

price.description: The base price of the medicine.

discountPercentage.description: The discount percentage applied to the medicine, if any.

categoryId.description: Reference to the MedicineCategory this medicine belongs to. (Relationship: MedicineCategory 1:N Medicine)

genericMedicineIds.description: References to alternative generic medicines. (Relationship: Medicine N:N Medicine - generic alternatives)

Prescription.title: Prescription
Prescription.description: Stores details of a medical prescription, which can be uploaded by users or issued by doctors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prescription entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this prescription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Prescription)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor who issued this prescription, if applicable. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Prescription)

imageUrl.description: URL to the image of the uploaded prescription.

voiceNoteUrl.description: URL to an uploaded voice note for the prescription.

issueDate.description: The date when the prescription was issued.

expirationDate.description: The date when the prescription expires.

diagnosis.description: The diagnosis for which the prescription was given.

medicineDetails.description: Textual details of the prescribed medicines and dosage.

prescriptionPdfUrl.description: URL to a PDF version of the prescription.

MedicineReminder.title: MedicineReminder
MedicineReminder.description: Manages reminders for a user to take or refill a specific medicine.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicineReminder entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set the reminder. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MedicineReminder)

medicineId.description: Reference to the Medicine for which the reminder is set. (Relationship: Medicine 1:N MedicineReminder)

scheduleTime.description: The time of day for the reminder (e.g., '08:00', '14:00').

frequency.description: How often the reminder should occur (e.g., 'Daily', 'Twice a day', 'Weekly').

startDate.description: The date from which the reminder should start.

endDate.description: The date until which the reminder should continue.

status.description: The current status of the reminder (e.g., 'Active', 'Completed', 'Paused').

dosage.description: The dosage instructions for the medicine.

Lab.title: Lab
Lab.description: Represents a diagnostic laboratory offering various medical tests.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lab entity.

name.description: The name of the diagnostic lab.

address.description: The physical address of the lab.

contactInfo.description: Contact information for the lab (e.g., phone number).

imageUrl.description: URL to the lab's logo or image.

verifiedStatus.description: Indicates if the lab is verified by the platform.

LabTest.title: LabTest
LabTest.description: Defines a specific type of laboratory test available for booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabTest entity.

name.description: The name of the lab test (e.g., 'Blood Test', 'Full Body Checkup').

description.description: A detailed description of what the test entails and its purpose.

price.description: The price of the lab test.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the test.

prerequisites.description: Any specific requirements before the test (e.g., 'Fasting required').

TestBooking.title: TestBooking
TestBooking.description: Records a user's booking for a lab test, including collection details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestBooking entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who booked the test. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TestBooking)

labTestId.description: Reference to the LabTest being booked. (Relationship: LabTest 1:N TestBooking)

labId.description: Reference to the Lab where the test will be performed. (Relationship: Lab 1:N TestBooking)

bookingDate.description: The date and time when the test was booked.

collectionDate.description: The scheduled date and time for sample collection.

collectionAddressId.description: Reference to the Address for home sample collection, if applicable. (Relationship: Address 1:N TestBooking)

status.description: The current status of the test booking (e.g., 'Pending', 'Sample Collected', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

paymentStatus.description: The payment status of the booking (e.g., 'Paid', 'Pending', 'Refunded').

familyMemberId.description: Reference to a FamilyMember for whom the test is booked, if applicable. (Relationship: FamilyMember 1:N TestBooking)

TestReport.title: TestReport
TestReport.description: Stores the results and associated document for a completed lab test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestReport entity.

testBookingId.description: Reference to the TestBooking associated with this report. (Relationship: TestBooking 1:N TestReport)

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TestReport)

labId.description: Reference to the Lab that generated this report. (Relationship: Lab 1:N TestReport)

labTestId.description: Reference to the LabTest that was performed. (Relationship: LabTest 1:N TestReport)

reportUrl.description: URL to the downloadable PDF of the test report.

resultSummary.description: A summary of the test results.

issueDate.description: The date when the test report was issued.

DoctorNote.title: DoctorNote
DoctorNote.description: Stores notes recorded by a doctor during an appointment for patient health records.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DoctorNote entity.

appointmentId.description: Reference to the Appointment during which this note was taken. (Relationship: Appointment 1:N DoctorNote)

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom the note was recorded. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DoctorNote)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor who recorded the note. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N DoctorNote)

noteContent.description: The actual content of the doctor's note.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the note was created.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase order, primarily for medicines.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including discounts and delivery charges.

deliveryAddressId.description: Reference to the Address for delivery. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

prescriptionId.description: Reference to the Prescription associated with this order, if medicines require it. (Relationship: Prescription 1:N Order)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Details individual items within an order, linking to the specific medicine and its quantity/price.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

medicineId.description: Reference to the Medicine being ordered. (Relationship: Medicine 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the medicine ordered.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the medicine at the time of order.

discountApplied.description: The total discount applied to this item.

Delivery.title: Delivery
Delivery.description: Manages the delivery process for an order, including tracking and status updates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Delivery entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order being delivered. (Relationship: Order 1:N Delivery)

trackingNumber.description: The unique tracking number for the delivery.

carrier.description: The delivery carrier or service provider.

estimatedDeliveryDate.description: The estimated date and time of delivery.

actualDeliveryDate.description: The actual date and time of delivery.

liveTrackingUrl.description: URL for live tracking of the delivery.

status.description: The current status of the delivery (e.g., 'Out for Delivery', 'Delivered', 'Attempted').

HealthTip.title: HealthTip
HealthTip.description: Provides daily health advice and tips to users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthTip entity.

title.description: The title of the health tip.

content.description: The full content of the health tip.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image accompanying the health tip.

publishDate.description: The date when the health tip was published.

category.description: The category of the health tip (e.g., 'Nutrition', 'Fitness', 'Mental Health').

WellnessContent.title: WellnessContent
WellnessContent.description: Stores various types of wellness-related content, such as yoga videos, diet plans, and care guides.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WellnessContent entity.

title.description: The title of the wellness content.

description.description: A brief description of the wellness content.

contentType.description: The type of wellness content (e.g., 'Yoga Video', 'Diet Plan', 'Pregnancy Care Guide', 'Mental Health Tip', 'Old Age Care Guide').

contentUrl.description: URL to the actual content (video link, PDF link, article link).

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image for the content.

tags.description: Keywords or tags associated with the content for search and filtering.

Offer.title: Offer
Offer.description: Represents promotions, discounts, and coupons for medicines or services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Offer entity.

description.description: A description of the offer details.

validFrom.description: The start date and time for the offer validity.

validTo.description: The end date and time for the offer validity.

minOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required to avail the offer.

applicableMedicineIds.description: References to specific Medicines to which this offer applies. (Relationship: Offer N:N Medicine)

applicableCategoryIds.description: References to MedicineCategories to which this offer applies. (Relationship: Offer N:N MedicineCategory)

Referral.title: Referral
Referral.description: Tracks user referrals, enabling a referral reward system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Referral entity.

referrerUserProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the referral. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Referral)

referredUserProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who was referred. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Referral)

referralDate.description: The date and time when the referral was made.

amountEarned.description: The monetary amount or reward earned by the referrer.

Pharmacy.title: Pharmacy
Pharmacy.description: Represents partner pharmacies or franchise locations that fulfill medicine orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pharmacy entity.

address.description: The physical address of the pharmacy.

contactInfo.description: Contact information for the pharmacy (e.g., phone number).

ownerUserProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this pharmacy, if it's a partner/franchise. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Pharmacy)

Disease.title: Disease
Disease.description: Information about various diseases, used by the AI Health Assistant for symptom checking and basic suggestions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Disease entity.

name.description: The common name of the disease.

description.description: A detailed description of the disease.

symptoms.description: A list of common symptoms associated with the disease.

commonMedicineIds.description: References to common Medicines used to treat this disease. (Relationship: Disease N:N Medicine)

Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Information about hospitals, primarily for the emergency services feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Hospital entity.

address.description: The physical address of the hospital.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the hospital's location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the hospital's location.

phone.description: The emergency contact phone number for the hospital.

emergencyServicesAvailable.description: Indicates if the hospital offers emergency services.

definition.description: Stores core user profile data. The document ID (`userProfileId`) directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. Includes the 'id' field in document which is also 'userProfileId'.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user's profile, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Private family member profiles associated with a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: familyMemberId
params.description: The unique identifier for a family member.

definition.description: Private address details for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier for an address.

definition.description: Publicly viewable doctor profiles. The 'id' field in the document is expected to be the 'userProfileId' (Firebase Auth UID) of the doctor, allowing direct management by the doctor. Includes 'verifiedStatus' for filtering and trust features.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctor, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Global list of medical specializations. Publicly readable for all users; write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: specializationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a medical specialization.

definition.description: Global list of consultation types (e.g., 'Chat', 'Audio Call', 'Video Call'). Publicly readable for all users; write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: consultationTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a consultation type.

definition.description: Time slots offered by a specific doctor. Publicly readable for users to discover and book, but only manageable by the 'doctorId' owner. Includes denormalized 'doctorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the doctor offering the time slot.

params.name: timeSlotId
params.description: The unique identifier for a time slot.

definition.description: Records a scheduled consultation. Accessible by both the 'userProfileId' (customer) and 'doctorId' (doctor), which are denormalized in the document for authorization independence and QAP-compliant listing for both parties.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for an appointment.

definition.description: Global list of medicine categories. Publicly readable for all users; write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a medicine category.

definition.description: Publicly viewable details of medicines available in the online store. Includes 'categoryId' for filtering and 'genericMedicineIds' for suggestions.

params.name: medicineId
params.description: The unique identifier for a medicine.

definition.description: Private medical prescriptions belonging to a user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: prescriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a prescription.

definition.description: Private medicine reminders set by a user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'medicineId' for authorization independence and context.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: reminderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a medicine reminder.

definition.description: Publicly discoverable diagnostic laboratories. Includes 'verifiedStatus' for filtering and trust.

params.name: labId
params.description: The unique identifier for a laboratory.

definition.description: Publicly viewable details of lab tests available for booking. Includes 'price' and 'prerequisites'.

params.name: labTestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a lab test.

definition.description: Records a user's lab test booking. Accessible by both 'userProfileId' (customer) and 'labId' (for lab staff/owner), both denormalized in the document for authorization independence and QAP-compliant listing.

params.name: testBookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a test booking.

definition.description: Private test reports belonging to a user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId', 'labId', and 'labTestId' for authorization independence and context.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: testReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a test report.

definition.description: Private doctor's notes for a specific user, created during an appointment. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'doctorId' for authorization and context. Primarily owned by the user.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a doctor's note.

definition.description: Private purchase orders placed by a user. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for an order.

definition.description: Individual items within a user's order. Includes denormalized 'userProfileId' and 'orderId' for authorization independence, following hierarchical path ownership.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's profile.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for an item within an order.

definition.description: Delivery details for an order. Accessible by 'userProfileId' (customer) and potentially by 'pharmacyId' (for pharmacy staff/owner). 'userProfileId', 'orderId', and 'pharmacyId' (if applicable) are denormalized for authorization independence and QAP-compliant listing.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a delivery.

definition.description: Publicly accessible daily health tips. Read-only for all users; write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: tipId
params.description: The unique identifier for a health tip.

definition.description: Publicly accessible wellness-related content (e.g., yoga videos, diet plans). Read-only for all users; write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a piece of wellness content.

definition.description: Publicly viewable promotions, discounts, and coupons. Read-only for all users; write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: offerId
params.description: The unique identifier for an offer.

definition.description: Tracks referral system entries. Accessible by both the 'referrerUserProfileId' and 'referredUserProfileId', which are denormalized in the document for authorization independence and QAP-compliant listing for both parties.

params.name: referralId
params.description: The unique identifier for a referral.

definition.description: Publicly discoverable pharmacies. Manageable by the 'ownerUserProfileId' (the Firebase UID of the owner), which is a field in the document, or by administrators.

params.name: pharmacyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a pharmacy.

definition.description: Publicly accessible information about various diseases. Read-only for all users; write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: diseaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a disease.

definition.description: Publicly accessible information about hospitals, primarily for emergency services. Read-only for all users; write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: hospitalId
params.description: The unique identifier for a hospital.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for identifying users with administrative privileges. The document ID ('adminUserProfileId') is the Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) through existence checks in security rules.

params.name: adminUserProfileId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user with administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, QAP-compliant list operations, and clear structural segregation as per the core design principles. This ensures robust security rules, efficient querying, and simplified debugging.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization Strategy (Strategy A):**
1.  **User-Owned Subcollections:** For entities like `FamilyMember`, `Address`, `Prescription`, `MedicineReminder`, `TestReport`, `DoctorNote`, `Order`, and `OrderItem`, which are direct subordinates of a `UserProfile`, the `userProfileId` (which corresponds to `request.auth.uid`) is explicitly denormalized into each document within the subcollection. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the parent `userProfiles/{userProfileId}` document in security rules. For example, a rule for `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/addresses/{addressId}` can simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId`, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic.
2.  **Shared/Cross-Entity Access Collections:** For entities like `Appointment`, `TestBooking`, `Delivery`, and `Referral`, which require access from multiple parties (e.g., user and doctor, user and lab, user and delivery driver, referrer and referred user), all relevant `userProfileId`s or `ownerId`s (`doctorId`, `labId`, `pharmacyId`, `referrerUserProfileId`, `referredUserProfileId`) are explicitly denormalized into the top-level document. This design ensures that security rules can evaluate access permissions solely based on the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, completely avoiding expensive and transaction-breaking `get()` operations. For `TimeSlot`s, the `doctorId` is denormalized into the `TimeSlot` document, as it's a subcollection of a doctor.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation (Strategy B):**
1.  **Publicly Accessible Data:** Core application entities that are global and read-only for most users, such as `Doctor`, `Specialization`, `ConsultationType`, `MedicineCategory`, `Medicine`, `Lab`, `LabTest`, `HealthTip`, `WellnessContent`, `Offer`, `Disease`, `Hospital`, are placed in top-level collections. These collections will typically have permissive read rules (`allow read: if true;`) for discovery and browsing, while write access is restricted to authenticated administrators. This structural segregation ensures that sensitive data is not mixed with public data, simplifying security rules and preventing accidental exposure.
2.  **Efficient Querying:** The denormalization of authorization fields (e.g., `userProfileId`, `doctorId`, `labId`, `pharmacyId`, `referrerUserProfileId`, `referredUserProfileId`) in relevant collections (e.g., `appointments`, `testBookings`, `deliveries`, `referrals`) allows for efficient and secure `list` queries. Clients can perform queries like `db.collection('appointments').where('userProfileId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `db.collection('appointments').where('doctorId', '==', request.auth.uid)` without the security rules acting as filters, as the `where` clause directly matches the intended access pattern. The rules will then simply verify `resource.data.userProfileId == request.auth.uid` or `resource.data.doctorId == request.auth.uid`.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims) & Access Modeling (Strategy C):**
*   **Role-Based Access:** Administrative roles are managed through dedicated 'existence collections' like `/admin_roles/{adminUserProfileId}`. Access can be granted by checking the existence of a document for the `request.auth.uid` in this collection, adhering to the 'Existence over Content' principle. Similarly, doctor and pharmacy owner roles are established by their `userProfileId` being the `id` of a `Doctor` document or the `ownerUserProfileId` of a `Pharmacy` document, simplifying ownership checks without custom claims.
*   **Hierarchical Paths for User Data:** User-specific data is structured under `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/...`, providing a clear, secure, and intuitive ownership model. This aligns with standard Firebase security best practices for private user data.

Overall, this structure prioritizes secure-by-design principles, facilitates straightforward security rule implementation by making authorization context explicit in each document, and supports the application's varied access patterns for users, doctors, labs, and administrators.
